rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LLOYD ET AL VS. CRANE CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 9, 2017 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant Crane Co.' motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues nos. 1-4, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Lloyd was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant and that such exposure falls outside the waiver stated in plaintiffs' complaint. Mr. Lloyd's deposition testimony (as to the bonnet gaskets removed from, and installed onto, Crane valves) and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Even if defendant did sustain its initial burden, Mr. Lloyd's deposition testimony and declaration and the testimony of William McLean and Anthony Pantaleoni (Exhibits I, J, K) create a triable issue whether Mr. Lloyd was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Lloyd's increased risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to summary adjudication issue no. 5 (punitive damages), defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant sustained its initial burden, plaintiffs' evidence (Exhibits G, I, K, L, R, T, BB) creates a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration, plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage and the Court cannot conclude that no reasonable jury could find plaintiffs' evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) (Tentative ruling Continued, see next entry)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LLOYD ET AL VS. CRANE CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Crane Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Crane Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (PART 2 OF 2) (Tentative ruling, continued from previous entry) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT STORMO ET AL VS. FLOWSERVE US INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Ameron International Corporation'S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 9, 2017 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Ameron International Corp.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue of exposure pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Mr. Stormo's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Stormo's declaration are overruled. The Court did not consider the declaration of Charles Ay. Plaintiffs' section 437c(h) continuance request was solely based on Mr. Stormo's declaration and the order granting plaintiffs' request limited supplemental filings to addressing that declaration. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Stormo. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
